title: Hourly, weekly, monthly, and yearly pay
topic_path: Hourly, weekly, monthly, and yearly pay

assessment_items.question_content:
You are paid $\$10$ an hour and you work $40$ hours per week. 

**What is your yearly pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: First, we need to calculate the weekly pay by multiplying **hourly pay** by the **number of hours** worked.
$\$10 \text{ per hour} \times 40 \text{ hours} = \$400 \text{ per week}$
Next, we can calculate the yearly pay by multiplying **weekly pay** by $52$ (number of weeks in a year).
$\$400 \text{ per week} \times 52 \text{ weeks} = \$20{,}800 \text{ per year}$
Your yearly pay is $\$20,800$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You're offered $\$15$ per hour with a $20$ hour working week.

**What would be your yearly salary?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: First, we need to calculate the weekly pay by multiplying **hourly pay** by the **number of hours** worked.
$\$15 \text{ per hour} \times 20 \text{ hours} = \$300 \text{ per week}$
Next, we can calculate the yearly pay by multiplying **weekly pay** by $52$ (number of weeks in a year).
$\$300 \text{ per week} \times 52 \text{ weeks} = \$15{,}600 \text{ per year}$
Your yearly salary would be $\$15{,}600$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You'll receive  $\$20$  per hour for $30$ work hours per week.

**What will your annual salary be?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: First, we need to calculate the weekly pay by multiplying **hourly pay** by the **number of hours** worked.
$\$20 \text{ per hour} \times 30\text{ hours} = \$600 \text{ per week}$
Next, we can calculate the yearly pay by multiplying **weekly pay** by $52$ (number of weeks in a year).
$\$600\text{ per week} \times 52 \text{ weeks} = \$31{,}200 \text{ per year}$
Your annual salary will be $\$31{,}200$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose you have a full-time job that pays $\$31{,}200$ annually. You work $40$ hours each week.

**What is your hourly wage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can find *hourly wage* by converting the annual (yearly) pay to weekly pay, and then calculating the hourly pay.

There are $52$ weeks in a year, so we calculate the weekly pay by dividing yearly pay by $52%$.

$\$31{,}200 \div 52 = \$600$ per week

Next, we take weekly pay and divide it by $40$ (number of hours worked) to calculate hourly pay.

$\$600 \div 40 = \$15$ per hour
Your hourly wage is $\$15$.

assessment_items.question_content:
You're offered a job that pays $\$26{,}000$ per year working $40$ hours a week.

**How much would you make per hour?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can find *hourly wage* by converting the annual (yearly) pay to weekly pay, and then calculating the hourly pay.

There are $52$ weeks in a year, so we calculate the weekly pay by dividing yearly pay by $52%$.

$\$26{,}000 \div 52 = \$500$ per week

Next, we take weekly pay and divide it by $40$ (number of hours worked) to calculate hourly pay.

$\$500 \div 40 = \$12.50$ per hour
You would make $\$12.50$ per hour.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are offered a $40$  hour a week job that pays $\$36{,}400$ annually.

**How much would your hourly wage be?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We can find *hourly wage* by converting the annual (yearly) pay to weekly pay, and then calculating the hourly pay.

There are $52$ weeks in a year, so we calculate the weekly pay by dividing yearly pay by $52%$.

$\$36{,}400 \div 52 = \$700$ per week

Next, we take weekly pay and divide it by $40$ (number of hours worked) to calculate hourly pay.

$\$700 \div 40 = \$17.50$ per hour
Your hourly wage would be $\$17.50$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Piper's job pays $\$250$ every week.

**What is her yearly salary?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find the yearly salary, we multiply the weekly salary by the number of weeks in a year.

There are $52$ weeks in a year, so we will multiply the weekly pay by $52$ to get yearly pay.

$\$250 \times 52 = \$13{,}000$ per year
Piper's yearly salary is  $\$13{,}000$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose you work part-time and earn $\$100$ per week.

**How much is your yearly pay?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To calculate the yearly pay, we'll need to multiply the weekly pay by the number of weeks in a year.

There are $52$ weeks in a year, so we will multiply the weekly pay by $52$ to get yearly pay.

$\$100 \times 52 = \$5{,}200$ per year
Your yearly pay is  $\$5{,}200$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Your wage is $\$500$ per week. 

**How much do you make in a year?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To calculate the yearly wage, we'll need to multiply the weekly wage by the number of weeks in a year.

There are $52$ weeks in a year, so we will multiply the weekly pay by $52$ to get yearly pay.

$\$500 \times 52 = \$26{,}000$ per year
You make  $\$26{,}000$ per year.

assessment_items.question_content:
Marley makes $\$16$ an hour and works $40$ hours a week. A new job offers her a salary of $\$35{,}000$ a year, working the same number of hours. 

**Which job pays more?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To figure out which job pays more, we need both wages to match: Either they both need to be *hourly* or both need to be *yearly*.

One way to do this would be to convert the current job to *yearly* pay:

Marley makes $\$16$ an hour and works $40$ hours a week. 

$\$16 \text{ per hour} \times 40 \text{ hours} = \$640 \text{ per week}.$

There are $52$ weeks in a year.

$\$640 \times 52  = \$33{,}280$

Comparing the two jobs side-by-side gives us the following:

Current job | $\text{\quad}$ | New job
:-: | :-: | :-:
$\$16$ per hour → $\$33{,}280$ per year   || $\$35{,}000$ per year
The **new job** pays more.

assessment_items.question_content:
You have a part-time job that pays $\$12$ an hour and you work $20$ hours a week. You get a new job offer that pays $\$14{,}000$ yearly.

**Which job pays more?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To figure out which job pays more, we need both wages to match. They either both need to be *hourly* or *yearly*.

One way to do this would be to convert the current job to *yearly* pay:

You currently  make $\$12$ an hour and work $20$ hours a week. 

$\$12 \text{ per hour}\times 20 \text{ hours} = \$240 \text{ per week}.$

There are $52$ weeks in a year. 

$\$240 \times 52 = \$12{,}480$.

Comparing the two jobs side-by-side gives us the following:

Current job | $\text{\quad}$ | New job
:-: | :-: | :-:
$\$12$ per hour → $\$12{,}480$ per year   || $\$14{,}000$ per year
The **new job** pays more.

assessment_items.question_content:
You work $30$ hours a week and make $\$15$ an hour. You're thinking of asking your boss for a raise to match what a different company pays, which is $\$20{,}000$ annually. 

**Which job pays more?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To figure out if this would be a raise, we need both wages to match. They either both need to be *hourly* or *yearly*.

One way to do this would be to convert the current job to *yearly* pay:

You currently  make $\$15$ an hour and work $30$ hours a week. 

$\$15 \text{ per hour} \times30 \text{ hours} = \$450 \text{ per week}$

There are $52$ weeks in a year. 

$\$450 \times 52 \text{ weeks in a year} = \$23{,}400$

Comparing the two jobs side-by-side gives us the following:

Current job | $\text{\quad}$ | Other job
:-: | :-: | :-:
$\$15$ per hour → $\$23{,}400$ per year   || $\$20{,}000$ per year
The **current job** pays more.
